rationale: The citing paper is an empirical sponsored-search/auction study, while the cited paper is a lab study on multistage consumer screening, so direct methodological or domain linkage is not evident from the notes alone. However, the citing note includes a behavioral explanation (recency-bias/choice-process effects), and the cited paper could plausibly be a mechanism source on information weighting in staged choice. Because the only matched evidence is a DOI-level reference entry with no local citation context, this is not safe to drop but also not clearly a strong-use citation.
